# temper

> A terminal-first CLI for checking domain availability across 30 TLDs in under 2 seconds. MCP-native so Claude, Cursor, and other AI assistants can search on your behalf.

## What it is

temper is an open-source command-line tool that queries RDAP (with WHOIS fallback) to check domain availability. It runs entirely on the user's machine with zero telemetry and no server dependencies.

- License: Apache 2.0
- Language: TypeScript, built on the Bun runtime
- Platforms: macOS, Linux
- Install: `brew install jongjinchoi/temper-domains/temper`
- Current version: 0.2.2
- Source: https://github.com/jongjinchoi/temper-domains
- Author: Jongjin Choi (https://jongjinchoi.com)

## Features

- 30 TLDs per search by default (59 with `--extended`, custom list via `--tlds`, or presets: `tech`, `popular`, `startup`, `cheap`)
- Interactive TUI (Ink + React) with vim-style navigation (j/k, /, a, s, h)
- MCP server mode for Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and Cline
- JSON output for shell piping (`--format json`)
- 7 built-in themes (5 dark, 2 light)
- Private by default — all queries run locally, no telemetry, no logs

## MCP tools

- `search_domain` — query multiple TLDs at once
- `suggest_domain` — generate prefix/suffix combinations
- `check_domain_availability` — single-domain check
- `whois_domain` — registrar, expiry, nameserver lookup
- `open_registrar` — open purchase page in the browser

## Commands

- `temper search <name>` — check 30 TLDs, Enter to buy
- `temper suggest <name>` — prefix/suffix brainstorm
- `temper whois <domain>` — RDAP-first registrar details
- `temper watch <domain>` — track availability, `temper list` to review
- `temper history` — interactive search history
- `temper mcp` — start the MCP stdio server

## Docs

- README: https://github.com/jongjinchoi/temper-domains/blob/main/README.md
- Releases / changelog: https://github.com/jongjinchoi/temper-domains/releases
- Live demo: https://temper-domains.vercel.app
